codeReview.summary: The governance packet is appropriately narrow: it adds the Lane 02 release-review and release-gate artifacts plus the explicit frontend no-op note, and its evidence ties Lane 02 back to the sealed preflight and Lane 01 packets without silently absorbing later review-follow-up commits into Lane 02 scope. The docs explicitly record the remaining orchestrator-only boundary—that the raw `origin/Factory-droid...HEAD` stack still contains later packets and therefore final branch refresh / merge handling must return upstream—while the packet re-runs the targeted Lane 02 regression pack, full `gate-fast`, and repo guard scripts, so the release gate does not rely on degraded runtime availability as a waiver for product proof.

summary: PASS. Lane 02 now has a review-ready release gate and cross-packet lineage audit, with the remaining merge/stack handling correctly deferred to the orchestrator.
